hero.title: Agent Hand
hero.subtitle: Agent Hand is an open-source Rust TUI that manages multiple AI coding agent sessions — Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cursor, Codex, Windsurf — from one tmux-backed dashboard with <50ms startup.
hero.tagline: Priority jump, fuzzy search, canvas workflow editor, AI session summarizer, and multi-tool hooks. Free core with Pro and Max tiers.

features.title: Key Features
features.subtitle: Free & open source — no account required

free.title: Status Detection
free.desc: Clear visual indicators show WAITING, RUNNING, READY, or IDLE status at a glance.

free.title: Priority Jump (Ctrl+N)
free.desc: Instantly jump to the most urgent session. Never miss a confirmation prompt again.

free.title: Fuzzy Switcher (Ctrl+G)
free.desc: Search and switch to any session in milliseconds. Type a few chars, jump directly.

free.title: PTY Monitoring
free.desc: Real-time pseudo-terminal usage tracking with system-wide gauge and warnings.

free.title: Groups & Labels
free.desc: Organize sessions by project with custom titles and colored labels.

free.title: tmux Isolation
free.desc: Dedicated tmux server won't touch your default tmux. Your configs, your workflow.

free.title: Multi-Tool Hooks
free.desc: Works with Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cursor, Codex, and Windsurf. Hooks auto-register on first run.

free.title: Chinese Language
free.desc: Auto-detects system locale. Full Chinese translation for CLI and TUI.

pro.title: Canvas Workflow Editor
pro.desc: Visual node graph to map session workflows. Drag nodes, draw edges, auto-layout. Auto-saves on exit.

pro.title: Session Relationships
pro.desc: Link sessions as parent-child, dependency, or collaboration. Dedicated relationship viewer with Ctrl+E.

pro.title: Skills Library
pro.desc: Browse, sync, and share reusable prompt skills. Git-based community repository.

pro.title: Sound Notifications
pro.desc: Audio alerts when sessions need attention. Cross-platform with configurable sound packs.

pro.title: Session Resume
pro.desc: Resume AI sessions across restarts. Auto-detects tool session IDs for Claude, Gemini, Cursor.

pro.title: Remote Sharing
pro.desc: Share live terminal sessions via WebSocket relay. Read-only or read-write mode with auto-expire.

max.title: AI Session Summarizer
max.desc: One-key AI summary of any session output. Supports DeepSeek, Claude, Ollama, and OpenAI-compatible providers.

max.title: Collaborative Context
max.desc: Capture relationship context with snapshots and annotations. Feed context into new sessions.

max.title: Remote Session Nodes
max.desc: Add canvas nodes for sessions on other machines. Cross-network relationship mapping.

preview.title: See It In Action
preview.alt: Agent Hand — AI terminal session manager dashboard showing Claude Code, Gemini CLI sessions with status indicators

install.oneLinerDesc: Works on macOS, Linux, and WSL. The script auto-installs tmux if needed.

install.wslNote: Recommended: use the macOS/Linux command inside
install.wslNoteSuffix: . Native PowerShell requires a POSIX layer with tmux (e.g. MSYS2).

pricing.title: Simple Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Core features are free forever. Pro is one-time. Max is a subscription.

story.title: From the Creator
story.p1: In early 2025, I was juggling {highlight} simultaneously. Four terminal windows, each with 3-4 tmux panes. 'Did I already respond to that prompt?' 'Which Claude is working on which task?' Wasting 10+ minutes just finding the right session.
story.p2: I tried the original agent-deck (Go) and loved the concept, but wanted better performance, more features, and cleaner integration. Agent Hand was born — a Rust rewrite that keeps what works and adds what I needed.
story.footer: The best tool is the one you'll actually use.

questions.a: Agent Hand is a fast, Rust-based terminal session manager (TUI) for AI coding agents like Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cursor, Codex, and Windsurf. It uses tmux for isolation, letting you manage 5+ concurrent AI sessions from one dashboard with status detection, priority jump, fuzzy search, a canvas workflow editor, and an AI session summarizer.

questions.q: How do I install Agent Hand?
questions.a: Run: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/weykon/agent-hand/master/install.sh | bash — works on macOS, Linux, and WSL. Or build from source with cargo build --release.

Yes, the core session manager is free and open source (MIT) with status detection, priority jump, fuzzy search, multi-tool hooks, and Chinese language support. Pro ($19 one-time) adds the canvas workflow editor, session relationships, skills library, sound notifications, session resume, and remote sharing. Max ($9/month) adds the AI session summarizer, collaborative context capture, and remote session nodes.

questions.q: Does Agent Hand work with Claude Code?
questions.a: Yes. Agent Hand was originally built to manage multiple Claude Code sessions. It detects session status (Waiting, Running, Idle), lets you priority-jump to sessions needing input with Ctrl+N, and provides fuzzy search across all sessions with Ctrl+G.

questions.q: How is Agent Hand different from tmux?
questions.a: Agent Hand runs on top of tmux (using a dedicated server so it won't touch your config). It adds AI-agent-specific features: automatic status detection, priority jumping, session grouping, PTY monitoring, and a visual dashboard — things raw tmux doesn't provide.
